methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The accumulated weight of all valid signatures must be greater than or equal to the required threshold for the wallet configuration.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the system accepts a signature weight lower than the threshold, an attacker with partial access (or no access) could authorize transactions and drain wallet funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_signatureWeight(uint256 weight, uint256 threshold) public pure { assert(weight >= threshold); }

invariants.description: The cumulative native token value transferred by a session key across all calls in a payload must not exceed its predefined value limit.
invariants.attack_scenario: A compromised or malicious session key could drain the wallet's entire native token balance, ignoring its restricted value limit.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_sessionValueLimit(uint256 totalValueUsed, uint256 valueLimit) public pure { assert(totalValueUsed <= valueLimit); }

invariants.description: The nonce for a specific space must strictly increment by exactly 1 after a successful transaction execution to prevent replay attacks.
invariants.attack_scenario: If nonces do not increment correctly, an attacker could replay a signed transaction multiple times, potentially draining funds or repeating a swap.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_nonceIncrements(uint256 space, uint256 preNonce) public { _consumeNonce(space, preNonce); assert(readNonce(space) == preNonce + 1); }

invariants.description: A queued recovery payload must not be valid for execution until the required delta time has elapsed since its queuing timestamp.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker bypasses the timelock to instantly execute a recovery payload, preventing the legitimate owner from canceling the malicious recovery attempt.
invariants.invariant_logic: verified == true implies block.timestamp >= queuedAt + requiredDeltaTime
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_recoveryTimelock(bool verified, uint256 queuedAt, uint256 requiredDeltaTime) public view { if(verified) { assert(block.timestamp >= queuedAt + requiredDeltaTime); } }

invariants.description: The tracked usage amount for cumulative permission rules must only increase and cannot be decremented.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker finds a way to decrement their usage limit, allowing them to repeatedly extract value or perform restricted actions indefinitely.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_usageLimitMonotonicity(address wallet, bytes32 usageHash, uint256 newUsageAmount) public view { uint256 oldUsageAmount = getLimitUsage(wallet, usageHash); assert(newUsageAmount >= oldUsageAmount); }
